data.narrative:
Quantum Machines is a Series C quantum computing company that has raised $280M to date. The valuation universe comprises 11 total peers across quantum computing: three public companies (IonQ, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum) and eight private competitors including Quantinuum, IonQ, D-Wave Quantum, Rigetti Computing, and Infleqtion.

A peer-median approach proved inapplicable due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables within the narrow quantum computing sector—only five total sector peers exist, and none met the threshold of three datapoints within tightly-filtered valuation tiers. Stage-progression analysis, however, is applicable. Using total capital raised ($280M) as a valuation proxy and applying a 1.7x multiplier typical for Series C to subsequent-stage progression yields $476M as the implied valuation.

The triangulation analysis, constrained to a single applicable method, supports a valuation range of $405M to $547M, with $476M as the median. Key uncertainties include the scarcity of same-stage private comparables in quantum computing, which limits cross-validation across methods, and the wide dispersion in maturity and business model among the five identified sector peers. A deeper comparable-company analysis would require either relaxing stage filters or obtaining additional financial metrics from the private peer set to strengthen conviction in this range.
